core_mechanism: a sharp Brascamp-Lieb-type multilinear inequality that bounds a product/integral of functions coupled through linear maps, used to certify that a configuration can simultaneously satisfy a large fraction of linear constraints
brief_reason: The abstract states its existential threshold and algorithmic guarantees are all 'obtained through a novel application of a Brascamp-Lieb-type inequality in the MDS setting,' making that bound on coupled linear-constraint satisfaction (paired with list decoding of the dual code) the machinery the whole method rests on.
